Ad Text:
This isn’t a role where you build from scratch.

It’s where you take a high-performing, mature PPC channel and push it further, finding the next 1% of growth, the next test and the next competitive edge.

If you’re a paid search leader who still loves being close to the detail, but wants to operate at a strategic, commercial level, this is a rare opportunity to do both.

Why this role stands out
Own one of the largest acquisition channels in the business
Lead a high-performing team of 5 PPC specialists and managers
Manage enterprise level budgets with full commercial accountability
Partner directly with senior leadership to shape strategy
Help future-proof performance as AI reshapes search
You’ll join a collaborative, close-knit team that’s grown significantly and continues to invest in its performance capability. 

What you’ll be doing
This is a strategic leadership role, focused on performance, people, and progression.

Performance & strategy

Drive incremental growth across an already optimised channel
Build and deliver a clear testing and optimisation roadmap
Identify new opportunities across products, platforms and AI-led search
Leadership & development

Lead, mentor and elevate a team of PPC managers and specialists
Shift focus from execution to strategic thinking and innovation
Build capability across forecasting, testing and performance ownership
Commercial ownership

Manage and optimise multi-million dollar spend
Own forecasting, planning and performance outcomes
Translate results into clear commercial insights for leadership
Partnerships & influence

Lead relationships with Google and Microsoft
Represent PPC across the broader marketing function
Influence decision-making at a senior level
What success looks like
Delivering efficient, incremental growth across key channels
Driving new product adoption and testing opportunities
Building and executing a clear performance roadmap for each product stream 
What you’ll bring
Strong background in PPC / Paid Search (hands-on expertise)
Experience managing large-scale accounts and budgets
Proven leadership experience, developing high-performing teams
Strong commercial acumen in forecasting, efficiency, ROI
Ability to communicate complex performance insights clearly
The environment
Hybrid: 3 days in office (Toowong), 2 days from home
Highly collaborative team; in-person time matters
Strong internal growth and development opportunities
Gain exposure across broader digital channels including SEO and content
The opportunity
This role exists because the team is growing, not because someone left.
You’ll step into a business with strong foundations and be the person who takes performance to the next level.

Apply now or contact Michael Johns at Gybe Consulting, 0406 210 924, michaeljohns@gybeconsulting.com.au for a confidential conversation.

Company Overview

Docusign brings agreements to life. Over 1.5 million customers and more than a billion people in over 180 countries use Docusign solutions to accelerate the process of doing business and simplify people’s lives. With intelligent agreement management, Docusign unleashes business-critical data that is trapped inside of documents. Until now, these were disconnected from business systems of record, costing businesses time, money, and opportunity. Using Docusign’s Intelligent Agreement Management platform, companies can create, commit, and manage agreements with solutions created by the #1 company in e-signature and contract lifecycle management (CLM).

What you'll do

Docusign is in the midst of a significant transformation. We've evolved beyond e-signature into Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) — an AI-powered platform that helps organisations create, commit to, and manage agreements more intelligently. For our public sector customers, this means faster service delivery, stronger compliance, reduced risk, and better citizen outcomes.

We're looking for a Lead Solution Consultant to join our ANZ team with a dedicated focus on Australian Public sector accounts. You'll partner with our Enterprise sales team to solve complex government challenges by translating Public Sector initiatives, processes, and technology requirements into compelling IAM solutions.

This position is an individual contributor role reporting to the Manager, Solution Consulting.


Responsibility

Act as the technical lead on Federal Government and Public Sector accounts in partnership with Enterprise Sales across ANZ, owning the Technical strategy from discovery through to close

Conduct deep discovery workshops with executive and senior government stakeholders to understand organisational challenges, policy drivers, and technology landscapes

Translate complex government requirements — including compliance, security, accessibility, and interoperability needs — into tailored IAM solution designs

Inform account planning with strategic technical insights that shape the overall customer strategy and execution approach

Build and deliver compelling product demonstrations, whiteboard sessions, proof-of-concept engagements, and workshops customised to public sector use cases

Respond to RFPs, RFIs, and Tenders with clear, compliant, and persuasive technical narratives

Communicate recommendations with confidence using value messaging frameworks, storytelling, customer evidence, and visually compelling materials

Collaborate closely with Sales, Professional Services, Customer Success, Product, Marketing and Leadership to maximise impact across customer engagements

Champion Docusign as a trusted, secure, and effective partner for government agreement processes

Stay current on public sector technology trends, government digital transformation programs, and relevant regulatory/compliance frameworks (e.g., PSPF, IRAP, ISM, NZ GCDO guidelines)

Share insights, learnings, and reusable assets across the team and broader organisation to drive scale and collaboration

Job Designation

Remote: Employee is not required to be in or near an office frequently and works from a designated remote work location for the majority of the time.

Positions at Docusign are assigned a job designation of either In Office, Hybrid or Remote and are specific to the role/job. Preferred job designations are not guaranteed when changing positions within Docusign. Docusign reserves the right to change a position's job designation depending on business needs and as permitted by local law.

What you bring

Basic

5+ years of experience in a pre-sales / solution consulting role selling SaaS solutions into enterprise accounts or Public Sector

Demonstrated ability to engage with senior stakeholders including C-suite and Senior Executive Service (SES) equivalents

Strong verbal and written communication skills with the ability to simplify complex technical concepts for non-technical audiences

Bachelor's degree

Reside in Canberra to maintain high-frequency engagement with key federal government stakeholders and internal leadership

Be able to travel (approximately 20% is expected)

Preferred

8+ years of pre-sales experience, ideally with exposure to Australian or NZ public sector customers

Understanding of government procurement processes, including panel arrangements, whole-of-government agreements, and tender/RFP cycles

Familiarity with Australian Government security frameworks (PSPF, ISM, IRAP) and the ability to articulate platform compliance positioning

Eligibility to obtain and maintain an Australian Government security clearance (Baseline minimum; NV1 desirable)

Experience with enterprise integration concepts including REST APIs, SOAP APIs, integration patterns, and familiarity with platforms such as SAP, ServiceNow, Microsoft, and Salesforce

Ability to articulate the value of AI in the context of technology solutions, with a focus on defining ROI and responsible AI use in government

Experience creating technical business documentation such as solution designs, workflow/process diagrams, proposals, and SOWs

Familiarity with web-based technologies including HTML, XML, and structured data

Familiarity with agreement lifecycle concepts — creation, negotiation, execution, and management

Post-graduate degree or continued formal education in relevant or adjacent fields

Life at DocuSign

Working here

Docusign is committed to building trust and making the world more agreeable for our employees, customers and the communities in which we live and work. You can count on us to listen, be honest, and try our best to do what’s right, every day. At Docusign, everything is equal.

We each have a responsibility to ensure every team member has an equal opportunity to succeed, to be heard, to exchange ideas openly, to build lasting relationships, and to do the work of their life. Best of all, you will be able to feel deep pride in the work you do, because your contribution helps us make the world better than we found it. And for that, you’ll be loved by us, our customers, and the world in which we live.

Accommodation

Docusign is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities in our job application procedures. If you need such an accommodation, or a religious accommodation, during the application process, please contact us at accommodations@docusign.com.

If you experience any issues, concerns, or technical difficulties during the application process please get in touch with our Talent organization at taops@docusign.com for assistance.

Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
Big transformation. Real impact. Immediate start.

This is your chance to step into a high-profile enterprise transformation program, helping deliver a major shift to a modern, cloud-based SAP platform. If you enjoy working in complex environments and know how to drive change that actually sticks this role is worth a look.

The Role

You'll take ownership of end-to-end change and communications delivery across a large-scale program, supporting the rollout of new systems, processes, and ways of working.

This is a hands-on role ideal for someone who enjoys being in the detail while still influencing at a senior level.

What you'll be doing

Drive change planning and delivery across the full project lifecycle
Develop practical, high-quality artefacts (impact assessments, comms plans, readiness plans)
Deliver clear, engaging communications to diverse stakeholder groups
Partner with stakeholders to embed change and improve adoption
Support business readiness, training, and transition activities
Work closely with senior leaders to influence and guide outcomes

The Environment

You'll be working on a major finance transformation program, moving from legacy systems to SAP S/4HANA Cloud and introducing more standardised, efficient ways of working.

SAP experience is highly regarded - but not essential.
What matters most is your ability to deliver change in complex environments and bring stakeholders on the journey.

What you'll bring

Strong experience in change management within complex programs
Proven ability to deliver end-to-end change outcomes (not just frameworks)
Confidence working with senior stakeholders and navigating ambiguity
Excellent communication skills - you know how to land a message
Experience in large-scale system or digital transformation environments
Exposure to ERP or SAP programs is a bonus, not a requirement

Why this role?

$123PH inc super
7-month contract with immediate impact and chance to extend
Hybrid working flexibility
Work on a program that will elevate your experience and profile

If you're a delivery-focused Change Analyst who can cut through complexity and drive real outcomes, apply now or reach out to ray.stewart@talentinternational.com

About Us

We're a Brisbane-based software company, delivering software solutions to the travel sector at large for over 25 years.

Our analytics platforms drive decision-making for travel sector clients worldwide.

We are a measured company that values professionalism, providing quality services whilst enjoying work life balance and looking to tread lightly on the planet.

The Role

We're looking for a sharp, technically capable Business Analyst to work closely with our Software Development Manager and act as a link between our customers and development team.

This is not a passive documentation role, you'll be expected to think, challenge, clarify, and drive outcomes.

What You'll Be Doing

Engage directly with customers to understand business needs and translate them into clear, actionable requirements

Analyse data and workflows to identify problems and opportunities for improvement

Produce high-quality functional specifications and supporting documentation for developers

Support the development lifecycle from concept through delivery and release

Provide ongoing customer support and issue resolution

Contribute to team coordination and operational management tasks

What You Bring

A Bachelor degree within the Information Technology domain

3-4 years of Business Analyst experience in a software development environment

Solid database skills (SQL proficiency key)

Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving capability

Proven experience in requirements gathering and documentation in a software environment

Confidence working with both technical teams and non-technical stakeholders

Ability to manage multiple priorities without losing attention to detail

Highly Regarded

Experience in the travel industry

Familiarity with Global Distribution Systems (GDS, Amadeus, Travelport and Sabre)

Exposure to data analytics platforms or reporting systems

Why This Role

Work on globally relevant products from Brisbane

Join a team that delivers in a competitive international market

Real ownership and influence, your work directly shapes the product evolution

Tight-knit, technically strong team with minimal bureaucracy

Apply

If you're someone who can cut through ambiguity, ask the right questions, and turn complexity into clarity, this role will suit you.

Please send your CV and a brief cover note outlining relevant experience.

Some are documentation-heavy, slow-moving, and sit far away from actual delivery.

This isn’t one of those.

We're looking for a digitally focused Business Analyst who can operate close to the product, work hands-on with delivery teams, and play a key role in shaping and releasing customer-facing platforms for our retail client.

Category:

Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
The role



The Business Analyst partners with business and technology stakeholders to understand business goals, analyse problems and opportunities, and translate needs into clear requirements, early designs, and prototypes. The role supports the delivery of user-centred digital solutions by shaping product and process improvements, validating concepts early, and enabling efficient delivery through well-defined user stories, acceptance criteria, and solution options.



The team



APRA's Technology team delivers, maintains, and enhances essential information technology services that support the organisation's productivity, strategic objectives, and business unit priorities.

Technology partner with stakeholders to design, implement and maintain IT solutions that meet the needs of the organisation and ensuring these are secure and aligned with APRA's technology architecture.

Key accountabilities


Build strong relationships with business stakeholders to quickly understand objectives, pain points and desired outcomes, and identify where digital products and solutions can add value.

Translate business needs into functional and non-functional requirements, ensuring they are testable, feasible and aligned to outcomes.

Produce high-quality analysis artefacts such as user stories, acceptance criteria, process maps, and impact assessments to support delivery.

Facilitate solution option analysis including costs, benefits, risks, dependencies, and delivery impacts to inform decision-making.

Collaborate with business and technical teams to create early design concepts (e.g., wireframes, workflow diagrams, low-fidelity screens).

Develop and iterate prototypes to validate requirements, user journeys, and usability with stakeholders and end users early in the lifecycle.

Coordinate and support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) planning and execution, including test scenarios, defect triage, and acceptance sign-off.

Develop and maintain system, training, and user support resources to drive adoption and meet user needs and expectations.

Communicate changes clearly and tailor messages and materials to different audiences, including non-technical stakeholders.




About you


Demonstrated experience as a Technical Business Analyst working in digital delivery environments, with strong capability across discovery, requirements elicitation and high‑quality documentation.

Strong experience delivering digital solutions across platforms such as Microsoft 365, Power Platform, ServiceNow and Oracle Fusion, with hands‑on technical understanding to shape solution design, integrations and delivery outcomes.

Experience facilitating user research, journey mapping and applying human‑centred design approaches.

Proven experience delivering initiatives across multiple digital domains, including web platforms, workflow, automation and enterprise business products.

Solid understanding of end-to-end product lifecycle, digital architecture, agile methodologies and IT service delivery best practices.

Excellent facilitation, communication, negotiation and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence outcomes and gain agreement across diverse business and technical stakeholders.

Demonstrated ability to manage priorities, driving outcomes in an agile, business-focused, collaborative environment.




To work with us you need to be an Australian Citizen and have the ability to gain a Defence Clearance.



About APRA

Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA) was established in 1998 as an independent statutory authority that supervises almost 1,200 financial institutions that manage $8.6 trillion in assets for Australians across the banking, insurance and superannuation sectors.

In overseeing the safety, competitiveness and stability of the financial system, we seek to recruit, develop and retain highly skilled professionals, who want to help shape financial services and protect the financial wellbeing of the Australian community. Our employee base of almost 900 come predominantly from the commercial financial services industry or other government agencies; as such, we have the feel of a small corporate organisation that can work flexibly and with agility.

At APRA, we're committed to providing an inclusive workplace where everyone belongs, feels valued and respected. We aspire to attract and foster diversity of background, thought, and experience, recognising that a broad range of perspectives, approaches and ideas makes us stronger, and better enables us to meet our obligation to protect the financial wellbeing of the Australian community. If you need any adjustments during the recruitment process, please inform at application stage so we can do our best to accommodate your requirements.

Your Next Great Opportunity: Making a Global Impact in Regenerative Medicine

At Maverick Bio, we are not just building a business, we are saving and improving lives. Our innovative biomaterials drive advances in regenerative medicine and medical devices worldwide.

As our Finance Business Partner, you will play a pivotal role supporting performance across our manufacturing and operations. Partnering closely with operational and leadership teams, you will provide costing insight, drive performance, and enable data-led decision-making as the business continues to grow.

Location: This role can be based in our Dubbo or our Brisbane Office (Eight Mile Plains - parking included).

Why Join Maverick Bio?

Real Impact, Unexpected Place

Contribute to life-saving procedures and ground-breaking health research
Make a difference in a business whose products impact lives globally

Real People, Real Work

Join a down-to-earth, collaborative team that values initiative and action
Experience the freedom to think deeply, influence decisions, and drive real outcomes

Real Growth

Partner closely with leaders
Shape costing, reporting, and performance as the business scales

What You Will Do

Partner with Operations teams and leaders to provide financial support
Analyse business and operational data to provide insights that inform strategic decisions
Deliver high-quality reports and dashboards on performance
Design and maintain financial models and benchmarking tools
Own and maintain standard costing, including variance analysis and cost trends
Deliver cost analysis across materials, labour, overheads, and production inputs
Lead and support cost accounting, budgeting and forecasting processes
Manage inventory accounting, including stock provisions and reconciliations
Identify cost drivers, inefficiencies, and opportunities to improve operational performance
Identify and implement ERP (Business Central) and data system improvements
Lead cross-functional improvement initiatives using lean methodologies
Advise the CEO, Head of Finance, and leadership team on performance-related projects

What We Are Looking For

Minimum 5 years’ experience in Finance Business Partnering, management accounting, or operational finance
Experience in manufacturing, supply chain, or a product-based environment
Strong business partnering capability with the ability to influence and build financial capability, elevating decision-making across non-financial stakeholders
Strong analytical and data interpretation skills
Expertise in financial modelling, cost analysis, and performance management
Solid understanding of inventory accounting and standard costing
Proficiency in ERP systems (Business Central), Power BI, and management reporting tools
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics or related field

Desirable

CPA/CA/CFA or Master’s degree
Experience in data visualisation, automation, or predictive analytics
Exposure to benchmarking and performance analysis
Proven experience leading change and cross-functional improvement projects

Who You Are

Commercially minded with a strong sense of ownership and accountability
Comfortable operating in a hands-on, operational environment
Analytical and curious, with the ability to translate data into practical recommendations
Confident engaging and influencing stakeholders across the business
Structured and detail-oriented, while maintaining a focus on outcomes
Motivated by continuous improvement and driving better business performance

Ready to Take the Lead?

Join a company that embraces innovation, fosters growth, and makes a real difference globally.

Click Apply Now to submit your resume and cover letter.

Have questions? Contact our friendly People and Culture Team on (02) 6885 1200 for a confidential conversation.

Equal Opportunity at Maverick Bio

We celebrate diversity and welcome applicants from all backgrounds. Maverick Bio is an Equal Opportunity Employer and encourages applications from individuals of all abilities, genders, races, religions, and orientations.

Ad Text:
Business Analyst Specialist

Contract to 27/11/2026 | Brisbane CBD | Hybrid working

We are partnering with a leading Queensland statutory authority to recruit experienced Principal Data Analyst. In this role, you will provide strategic advice on information management issues, strategies, frameworks and support models that will underpin best practice and effective lifecycle management of departmental records and information.

Key responsibilities:

Design, implement, and optimize document libraries, metadata structures, taxonomies, and SharePoint/Teams workspaces.

Create and maintain workflows, permissions, and access controls to ensure secure document collaboration.

Lead or assist in the migration of documents from legacy repositories (e.g. eDOCS) to Microsoft 365, ensuring minimal disruption and data integrity.

Map metadata and ensure appropriate classification, tagging, and storage of migrated content.

Develop, maintain and foster a commitment to quality customer service which you will demonstrate by providing advice on the development, design and operation of contemporary information management systems with a focus on the M365 platform.

Consult and participate in the development and implementation of policy, procedures, standards, information classification and labelling, user access controls, information sharing protocols and other necessary frameworks that will enable the effective operation of IM.

What we’re looking for?

5+ years of experience in Microsoft 365 (SharePoint, Teams, OneDrive) configuration and administration.

Experience with document management systems such as eDOCS.

Experience with cloud-based record management platforms such as RecordPoint.

Detailed knowledge of current and emerging Information Management trends, standards and practices including content service platforms, use of Microsoft platform and collaboration tools and managing their impact on IM.

Establishes and maintains co-operative working relationships with co-workers and stakeholders.

Demonstrated ability to work with a diverse set of stakeholders to raise departmental-wide awareness and adoption of IM standards, that influence the implementation of platforms including case management systems, electronic workflows, electronic document and records management systems.

Analyses information, problems, situations, practices or procedures to define the problem or objective.

Why apply through Lime Recruitment?

Enjoy two pay runs per week without contractor fees. As a Queensland-owned agency, we offer personalised service and are a Tier 1 supplier to the Queensland Government. Awarded Australia’s top-performing small agency in 2021 and 2022, with a finalist spot in 2023. Partner with Lime for genuine, experienced, friendly recruiters dedicated to securing the best opportunities for you. Apply now or contact  Ianthe Price on ianthe@limejobs.com.au to find out more!

We’re looking for a strategic and delivery-focused Senior Business Analyst to join a high-impact digital transformation program. Working across digital metering and process transformation initiatives, you’ll play a key role in shaping future-state processes, driving innovation, and ensuring solutions deliver real value for both the business and customers.

You’ll take end-to-end ownership of analysis activities—from early discovery and business case development through to delivery—partnering closely with stakeholders across business and technology teams. This includes facilitating workshops, gathering and defining requirements, supporting solution design, and contributing to change and business readiness activities.

Key Responsibilities:

Facilitate workshops and stakeholder engagement sessions across the organisation
Develop business cases, cost-benefit analysis, and ROI assessments

Key Experience:

This is a great opportunity for a proactive and analytical Business Analyst who thrives in fast-paced environments and is passionate about driving meaningful transformation and business outcomes.

Who are we? 
 
Standards Australia (SA) is the peak standards development organisation in Australia, with a rich history that dates back to 1922. Currently, we are undergoing a significant transformation and growth phase. In recent years, our workforce has doubled, and we now proudly employ over 200 professionals dedicated to helping shape Australia’s future and fulfilling our mission of empowering communities. 
 
Our organisation brings experts together to create national and international standards that serve as essential guidelines for products and services, ultimately benefiting the Australian community. Our vision is to be a global leader in providing trusted solutions that improve life today and tomorrow. 

About the role 

We are seeking a Portfolio Scheduling & Reporting Lead to join our Group Project Management Office (GPMO).

Reporting to the Head of GPMO, this role is responsible for maintaining the organisation’s enterprise project schedule and portfolio reporting. You will provide a consolidated view of project timelines, dependencies, risks and resource demand across strategic initiatives, enabling senior leaders to make informed decisions on delivery priorities.
  
What you’ll do

Maintain the enterprise master schedule across organisational projects
Identify and manage cross-project dependencies, sequencing risks and delivery impacts
Support Project Managers and business teams to develop aligned and realistic project schedules
Design and maintain portfolio reporting dashboards and executive reporting
Provide insights on milestones, resource demand and delivery progress
Partner with Finance to support portfolio budget tracking and forecasting
Provide leadership and oversight to the Portfolio Reporting Analyst
About you

You are an experienced PMO or portfolio professional with strong analytical and scheduling capability.

You will bring:

5+ years’ experience in PMO, portfolio scheduling or project controls roles
Experience managing complex multi-project schedules and portfolio reporting
Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate delivery data into clear insights
Experience using tools such as MS Project, advanced Excel, Power BI, Jira or Confluence
Experience working within enterprise PMO or programme governance environments
Culture & Benefits 

Standards Australia is proud to have been awarded the prestigious Employer of Choice Award for two consecutive years, 2023 and 2024. This recognition highlights our commitment to creating an exceptional workplace and fostering a culture of excellence. Our iCARE values form the basis of all we do - Integrity, Courage, Accountability, Respect, and Excellence. 
 
We offer a range of People Perks that support flexibility, wellbeing, career growth, and recognition - helping you thrive at work and beyond.

What's next? 
 
If Standards Australia sounds like the place for you, please submit your CV outlining your experience and suitability to this role.
Eligible applicants must be Australian Citizens, Permanent Residents or have legal rights to work in Australia. Shortlisted applicants will be contacted within 15 business days of their application. 

At Standards Australia, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and we are committed to fostering a working environment that values diversity, inclusion, and belonging. Our mission is to empower our communities, and in doing so, we strive to create an organisation that genuinely reflects and respects the diversity of the communities we serve; putting people at the heart of all we do. We welcome applications from people of all cultural and linguistic groups, people of all ages and genders, people of Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander background, people with a disability, and people from the LGBTQIA+ community. Should you need any adjustments to be made to our recruitment process, please contact recruitment@standards.org.au

Salary: Excellent base salary, super and benefits.
Ad Text:

What You’ll Be Doing

 

We’re looking for a curious and proactive Senior Advisor - First Line Risk to help us strengthen our enterprise-wide risk capability. You’ll sit at the heart of our Legal, Risk and Compliance team, partnering with leaders across two iconic businesses - Compare the Market and iSelect - to deliver smart, practical risk initiatives that genuinely lift how we operate.

       Oversee control testing and remediation to ensure risks are managed effectively
       Turn risk data and trends into clear insights and smart, targeted actions
        Lead engaging risk workshops that build understanding and support a positive risk culture

You’ll be joining a small, growing team in a developing risk environment, where there’s plenty of opportunity to shape how things are done and lots to build and improve.  This role is best suited to someone self-driven who enjoys rolling up their sleeves, is comfortable with ambiguity and thrives in an agile, evolving environment with lots of meaningful work to tackle.

We’re guided by three shared values that shape how we work, how we treat each other, and how we show up for our customers - Drive, Noble Intent and All Together.

 

Plus, we offer a range of great perks to help you thrive both in and out of work:

What Sets You Apart

 

You’re someone who brings energy, curiosity and a practical approach to risk - without overcomplicating things.  You’ll also have:

Wolters Kluwer’s Tax & Accounting division in APAC delivers authoritative political, tax and legal content to professional services firms and corporations across the region. The way these firms consume, integrate, and apply that content is changing rapidly. AI-powered workflows, agent-based systems, and new integration patterns like MCP (Model Context Protocol) are reshaping how professional content gets used in practice.

We’re hiring a Senior Product Manager to own the development of the next iteration of our content offerings. Champion innovation, customer-centricity, and operational excellence to deliver market-leading expert solutions for legal, accounting, and political information professionals in a market where technology and competition are ever-changing. You will drive customer and business value by leading the strategy, development, and evolution of CCH iKnowConnect and CCH Parliament, ensuring alignment to wider commercial strategies to drive revenue and renewals.

Key Responsibilities
Product Innovation

Work closely with the Associate Director, Content Solutions, to understand Wolters Kluwer’s strategic and commercial objectives and the evolving needs of tax, legal and political information markets.

Define, communicate and lead a compelling and innovative product vision and strategy that aligns with those objectives and needs, and delivers on our financial goals.

Champion the integration of emerging technologies to enhance product value and competitive differentiation, driving product adoption, usage and revenue growth.

Stay current on AI delivery patterns: LLM integration, retrieval-augmented generation, MCP, agentic workflows. Use that knowledge to drive innovation that doesn’t merely keep up but leapfrogs the competition. 
 

Customer & Market Insight

Deeply understand customer segments, pain points and workflow through direct customer engagement, research and data analysis in collaboration with the Go-To-Market (GTM) team. Run client discovery to understand use cases and business scenarios to inspire innovative product development, ensuring initiatives are grounded in validated customer problems, not assumptions or technology‑led solutions.

Use qualitative and quantitative insights to validate desirability, viability, and feasibility of new product ideas and enhancements before they enter the roadmap.

Ensure all new product ideas and enhancements drive commercial objectives. Sequence and prioritize client requests: what’s achievable now with existing infrastructure, what requires new investment, what needs a phased approach.

Understand market trends and competitor insights provided by the GTM team and how they impact current and future product development. Use those insights to ideate and innovate solutions to bring to market to drive revenue goals.
 

Product Development & Delivery

In consultation with the Associate Director, Content Solutions and the Commercial Manager (R&L), own the end-to-end product lifecycle, from ideation and MVP definition through to launch, ongoing optimisation, and eventual sunset, working closely with Product Owners.

Develop and maintain outcome-based product roadmaps to prioritise initiatives that drive measurable customer and commercial impact.

Ensure robust release strategies, including beta programmes, phased rollouts, and feedback loops.

Manage both an internal team of product specialists and a remote team of developers.

Develop and maintain relationships with WK’s global technology and development teams and the North American product teams to identify technology and product developments that may be adapted for the APAC market.

Collaborate cross-functionally with design, engineering, content, sales and GTM teams to deliver high-quality releases, launches and customer communications.

Conduct internal and customer-facing webinars to demonstrate product features and functionality. 
 

Commercial & Customer Success

Support the Commercial Manager (R&L) in annual, quarterly and monthly outcome-based planning, preparing business cases to secure internal capital funding for strategic initiatives (in consultation with the Associate Director, Content Solutions). Manage the capex and opex budget to maximize returns.

Work with the Associate Director, Content Solutions, and Commercial Manager (R&L) to set, track and report on clear success metrics (customer adoption, retention, NPS, revenue, etc) for all initiatives with accountability for closing the loop between delivery, customer outcomes, and commercial performance.

Monitor product performance, proactively identifying opportunities for improvement and intervening as needed.

Partner with Customer Success, Sales, and Marketing to ensure go‑to‑market readiness, support effective product launches, and drive sustained adoption, engagement, and customer advocacy. 

Manage regular upstream reporting requirements with the Commercial Manager (R&L) incorporating customer insight and adoption learnings alongside financial performance.
 

Team Leadership, Collaboration & Stakeholder Management

Foster a high-performing, collaborative product team culture, ensuring alignment to vision, goals, and values.

Engage with stakeholders across the business, including executive sponsors, content leaders, technology, and commercial teams to ensure product development is aligned with strategic goals.

Work collaboratively with commercial leaders in the software portfolio to identify innovation opportunities and integrate content solutions into software products. 
 

What Success Looks Like: 

Wolters Kluwer APAC is acknowledged as a market leader in delivering innovative solutions.

Customers and stakeholders clearly understand the value proposition and strategic direction of CCH iKnowConnect and CCH Parliament.

Roadmaps are executed effectively and on time, with the team focused on initiatives that drive renewal and new sales.

Product success is defined, measured, and communicated, with continuous improvement based on data and feedback. 
 

About You:

Proven experience in product strategy and management, ideally in B2B SaaS or a professional services technology space. Tax, accounting, or legal domain experience is a plus but not required if you bring strong professional services or fintech context.

Experience with AI-driven product development and AI implementation. You can talk to a client’s technology leadership about LLMs, APIs, retrieval-augmented generation, and agent architectures without reading from a slide. Familiarity with MCP or a track record of picking up new technical integration patterns quickly.

You’ve operated in a global, matrixed organization and know how to get things done when your success depends on teams in other time zones and other reporting lines. Demonstrated ability to inspire and lead a team when delivering complex projects.

You’re comfortable when the playbook doesn’t exist yet - clients are asking for things they can’t fully describe. You help them figure out what they actually need and then find a path to deliver it.

You have a passion for innovation which is tempered by strong strategic thinking, analytical and problem-solving skills, and a commercial approach. You are flexible in your approach and take initiative rather than wait to be asked. 

Demonstrated ability to define and articulate a clear strategic vision in ambiguous environments, and to translate that vision into commercially viable, revenue‑generating deliverables that drive sustainable business growth.

Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to engage with customers and multiple stakeholders in a complex business. 

Our Values

Wolters Kluwer continuously strives for an inclusive company culture in which we attract, develop, and retain high-performing, productive, engaged, and diverse talent to deliver on our strategy. As a global company, having a diverse workforce from different backgrounds, nationalities, races, genders, gender identities, ages, sexual orientations, physical disabilities, religions, expertise, and talents is of the utmost importance.

We pride ourselves on our culture, which promotes inclusion, accessibility and flexible working arrangements.

Culture and Benefits

We care for our people and a part of that we offer:

Flexible Working Arrangement – Work from Anywhere and Hybrid Working (promoting work life balance)

Learning and Development opportunities

Access to health and wellness programs

Parental leave benefits that exceed legislative requirements

The opportunity to work within a global organization with experienced leaders

You can learn more about what we do by visiting our:
 

Website : Wolters Kluwer Australia | Wolters Kluwer

Meet the People: How the People of Wolters Kluwer Strive to Be The Difference | Wolters Kluwer

To maintain a fair and genuine hiring process, we kindly ask that all candidates participate in interviews without the assistance of AI tools or external prompts. Our interview process is designed to assess your individual skills, experiences, and communication style. We value authenticity and want to ensure we’re getting to know you—not a digital assistant. Job Title: Growth Intelligence - Analytics and AI Lead
Category:
Management (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
About the Company 

SA Water is a successful, modern water utility wholly owned by the Government of South Australia. We employ more than 1,700 people across a broad range of disciplines who operate more than $14 billion worth of infrastructure to deliver essential water and sewerage services to more than 1.8 million South Australians.

We operate in a geographically and climatically diverse service area, with sites and locations from Ceduna through to Port Augusta, the Anangu Pitjantjatjara Yankunytjatjara (APY) Lands, across to the Riverland, down to Mount Gambier, and most places in between.

Our strength and expertise have been shaped by our history. For more than 165 years, South Australians have depended on us to innovate and deliver efficient and sustainable water services. With nationally and internationally recognised industry expertise, we’re focused on understanding and meeting our customers’ expectations.

As well as safely delivering smart and reliable services for our customers of today, we innovate to shape the water solutions of the future that will support thriving economies and healthy communities.

We are driven by our values; we value our people, we do work that matters, we keep it real, we know that our work has impact and we are future focussed. Staying true to our values ensures that we consistently deliver positive outcomes for all South Australians.

About the job  

The Growth Intelligence - Analytics and AI Lead plays a pivotal role in shaping how data insights and AI are used to drive smarter decision-making across Growth. Leading a small team of data experts and contractors, this role will work as a Data Product Owner and is responsible for delivering high-quality analytics, building strong data foundations and enabling scalable, AI driven solutions.  

Acting as the key interface between our transformation partner and internal teams, you’ll ensure initiatives are grounded in our data environment and aligned to business priorities.  

This is a hands-on leadership role focused on simplifying complexity, automating processes, accelerating data product delivery and embedding efficient repeatable ways of working that create real impact.  

This is a max-term opportunity until June 2027. 

About the Person

You’re a pragmatic and curious leader who understands how data and analytics come together in practice. With a strong background in analytics, reporting, data engineering, you’re comfortable navigating complex environments and translating between technical teams and business stakeholders.  

You don’t need to be an AI expert, but you have genuine interest in its potential and how it can be applied to solve real problems. You led from the front, bringing others with you, and are driven by improving how things work, whether that through automation, improve processes or deliver continuous value through smarter use of insights.  

You’re equally comfortable setting direction and rolling up your sleeves to make it happen.  

About Growth

The Growth group is at the heart of shaping South Australia’s future. This team plays a key role in supporting the state’s housing and infrastructure growth by delivering trusted water services that unlock land for development, create thriving new communities, and sustain existing regions. With a strong focus on single-point accountability, they ensure efficiency, consistent decision-making, and seamless connections for our customers. By bringing expertise and innovation together, they help drive smarter solutions and stronger partnerships that support South Australia’s growth for generations to come.

In addition, your responsibilities will include: 

Fostering trust with our people, customers and community by providing expert advice and service that ensure we effectively communicate and engage audiences with our business strategy and its delivery across South Australia, every day. 

You will: 

Lead, mentor, coach, guide the team to deliver data driven insights faster. 
Ensure the intelligence analysts, contractors and other personnel within the team achieve business objectives and goals within agreed timeframes. 
Provide timely guidance, feedback, coaching and training to help team members strengthen specific knowledge/skill areas needed to accomplish a task or solve a problem.
Lead initiatives to improve the maturity of domain data modelling, Growth Intelligence and Transformation.  
Review and determine suitability of new, and emerging AI technology, solutions/algorithms/models and recommend to be adopted by the Growth Group. 
Collaborate with teams across Growth, Planning, Systems, Customer and Technology to support integrated planning and intelligence data product delivery. 

Your success in this role will come from: 

Tertiary qualification in Data, AI, Information Technology, Business, or a related discipline.
Demonstrated experience in supporting the implementation of data solutions that align with business objectives. 
Effective communication and people skills including an ability to collaborate to deliver improved business outcomes.
Intermediate skill level in Microsoft Azure, Collibra, Microsoft SQL, data integration, data modelling, and data architecture.  
Ability to lead the team manage multiple tasks and deadlines within a continuously improving organization. 

To apply, click 'Apply'. Please submit and complete the application form, upload a cover letter and current resume. For enquiries please contact Steph Haines from the Talent Acquisition team steph.haines@sawater.com.au

Further information can be found in the attached PD - Growth Intelligence - Analytics & AI Lead

Location: Adelaide
Contract length: 12 months (definite extensions)
Daily rate: $750-900 per day including super

About

This organisation is undertaking a major uplift of its Microsoft 365 environment, focused on strengthening governance, improving content management, and enabling secure, scalable collaboration.

The current landscape is fragmented, with inconsistent processes and legacy platforms limiting efficiency, compliance, and the ability to adopt emerging capabilities such as GenAI. This program is designed to modernise the environment through clear governance frameworks, streamlined content management practices, and enhanced information security controls.

The role will play a key part in shaping how information is managed, accessed, and protected across the business, supporting both immediate operational improvements and long-term scalability.

Responsibilities

Lead business analysis activities across a Microsoft 365 governance and content management uplift program
Facilitate workshops with business and technical stakeholders to gather, challenge, and refine requirements
Capture current ("as is") processes and design future ("to be") states aligned to best practice
Translate business needs into clear, structured requirements and supporting artefacts
Identify opportunities for process improvement, standardisation, and automation
Support the development of governance frameworks, information architecture, and content management models
Contribute to business case development, benefits identification, and value realisation tracking
Work closely with delivery teams to ensure requirements are understood and implemented effectively
Support project lifecycle activities including planning, delivery, and operational handover

Criteria

Strong core Business Analysis capability, including requirements elicitation, process mapping, and stakeholder engagement
Experience working within complex IT environments, ideally on enterprise-wide transformation initiatives
Proven ability to facilitate workshops and communicate technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders
Experience with process improvement methodologies and identifying inefficiencies
Strong understanding of Microsoft 365 ecosystem (e.g. SharePoint, Teams, OneDrive, Power Platform)
Knowledge of information management practices, including governance, data classification, and retention
Experience working in Agile or hybrid delivery environments
Familiarity with regulatory and compliance considerations relating to data and information management
Ability to operate across both strategic and hands-on delivery activities

Why Apply

Opportunity to contribute to a high-impact, enterprise-wide transformation program
Work on modernising collaboration and information management practices at scale
Exposure to governance, security, and emerging technologies including GenAI readiness
Engage with a broad range of stakeholders across business and technology
Join a collaborative environment that values initiative, accountability, and continuous improvement


If the above seems like a good fit, please apply or email karim.romdhane@precisionsourcing.com.au and we will be in touch if your profile is suitable!

A lot of data science roles talk about impact.
This one measures it.

You’ll work on AI and GenAI features that sit directly in front of customers. Not internal dashboards. Not experiments that never ship. Real product functionality, where success is defined by whether it improves the experience and holds up in the real world.

This team is embedding ML and LLM capabilities into products used by thousands of customers. That means real constraints, real trade offs, and real accountability. You’ll operate in domains where correctness, trust, and compliance matter just as much as innovation.

They’ve moved past asking “can we build this?”
Now it’s “should we build this, and how do we prove it works?”

What you’ll be doing 

Frame hypotheses with product
Design experiments beyond launch
Prototype and ship GenAI/ML solutions
Take ideas from MVP to production
Apply NLP, retrieval, forecasting and recommendations
Define and track meaningful success metrics
Run offline and live experiments
Ship with ML engineers and frontend teams
Work in regulated, risk aware environments
Influence data science practices across the team
Who this suits
You’ve shipped customer facing ML or GenAI
You have hands on LLM experience
You think in hypotheses, not just outputs
You’re strong in Python and modern ML tooling
You can work effectively with engineers
You influence across product and stakeholders
You communicate clearly in complex environments
A PhD is welcome but not required. Demonstrated impact matters more.

What’s in it for you
Real products, real users, real impact
Strong ML and product peers
Influence how experimentation is done
Melbourne based, in person collaboration preferred
If you’re looking for a role where GenAI is actually in production, experimentation drives decisions, and your work reaches customers, this is worth a conversation.

Optimisation Specialist

As we continue to scale, we’re investing in how our technology supports the business to operate effectively into the future and deliver at pace. To support this, we’re looking for an Optimisation Specialist to join our Technology Services team.

This role sits within Technology Delivery and focusses on improving how systems, tools and workflows come together to support critical day-to-day operations across the Group.

It’s suited to someone who enjoys working closely with the business and helping optimise workflows so they are simpler, more effective, and well supported by our technology advancements, as we continue to expand into the McNab Group.

 

About the Role

Reporting to the Technology Delivery Manager, you’ll play a key role in supporting the uplift of technology and workflow initiatives across McNab, and the way we need to operate and communicate across functions as we scale.

You’ll work alongside business and technology teams to understand current workflows, identify friction points, and help shape practical improvements that align to broader technology priorities.

WorkCover Queensland is entering an exciting new chapter, with a renewed strategic focus and strong executive leadership driving our 2030 Strategy. As we deliver an enterprise-wide transformation, strong data management and governance will underpin our ability to make a lasting difference for Queenslander employers and employees.

Building WorkCover’s Data Future

As part of this transformation, WorkCover is establishing a new Data Office to uplift data maturity across the organisation. This collaborative, customer centric and outcomes focused function brings together specialist leads across governance and quality, working as one integrated team.

Moving away from a traditional centralised model, the Data Office operates through partnership and collaboration, embedding with business and delivery teams to enable trusted, high quality data that supports both business as usual excellence and future capability.

The Opportunity

Reporting to the Manager, Data Office, the Lead, Data Office Quality is accountable for ensuring WorkCover’s data is trusted, fit for purpose and aligned to organisational goals.

As the organisation’s data quality authority, you set direction, provide expert guidance and influence how quality is embedded throughout the data lifecycle. You work across business units and transformation teams to uplift data maturity, enable confident decision making and support WorkCover’s move towards a more reliable, future ready data ecosystem.

This role exists to build confidence in data, reduce risk and ensure quality is not an afterthought, but an embedded capability across the organisation.

In this role you will:

Lead day to day data quality operations, ensuring reliable and trusted data across transformation initiatives.
Define, embed and maintain data quality standards, frameworks and controls.
Act as the central escalation point for data quality issues, risks and exceptions.
Monitor, profile and report on data quality, identifying trends, risks and systemic issues.
Lead root cause analysis and guide teams to implement sustainable remediation.
Partner with delivery teams to ensure data products meet quality standards prior to release.
Collaborate with business, analytics and transformation teams to drive consistent quality outcomes.
Drive continuous improvement and uplift data quality maturity across the organisation.

About You

You are an experienced data quality leader with a strong transformation mindset and a passion for building confidence in data. You thrive on collaboration, bring structure to complexity and influence outcomes through expertise and engagement. You bring:

Extensive experience leading data quality in complex or regulated environments.
Strong knowledge of data quality frameworks, governance, privacy and regulatory controls.
Proven ability to diagnose issues and deliver sustainable remediation.
Confidence embedding quality practices into BAU operations and transformation initiatives.
Excellent communication skills across technical and non technical audiences.
A collaborative, coaching leadership style with a continuous improvement mindset.

Why Join Us

Our vision is to be the best workers’ compensation insurer, making a positive difference to people’s lives and keeping Queenslanders working. At WorkCover, we deliver with heart, own our outcomes, work best together and stay open to what’s possible - and we’re looking for people who share these values

When you join us, you’ll benefit from:

A purpose driven organisation where your work genuinely makes a positive impact on Queenslanders’ lives
A collaborative and supportive team environment that values empathy, trust and working together
Hybrid work arrangements that support flexibility and work–life balance
Personalised learning and development opportunities to support your growth and career progression
A holistic health and wellbeing program designed to support you both professionally and personally
Industry competitive salaries
A generous superannuation scheme, including co contribution
Study assistance to support ongoing education and professional development

You can find out more about what it’s like to work with us by visiting Working at WorkCover Qld.  

How do I apply?

If this sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you. Applications close Friday 8th May at 5pm. 

We are committed to ensuring WorkCover reflects the diversity of the Queensland community. We welcome applications from First Nations peoples, members of the LGBTQIA+SB community, people of all ages, people who are neurodivergent, people with disability, and people from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds.

Acceptance of applications submitted after advertising closes is at the discretion of the selection panel.

To ensure you have the best and most inclusive experience, please let us know if you require any reasonable adjustments or accommodations to help you confidently showcase your skills and potential. You can contact us at recruitment@workcoverqld.com.au or let our recruitment team know during your conversation with them.

This includes a Right to Work in Australia Check, Police Check, Education Check, Reference Check, and where appropriate a Visa Check. The information provided will be treated as confidential in accordance with the Information Privacy Act 2009 (Qld). All permanent appointments are subject to a probationary period in accordance with our policy.

Company Description


Supporting millions of Aussies since 1988 with low fees and competitive long-term performance. Profits back to members, not shareholders

Closing date: 20th May 2026 

Please note Rest does not accept speculative resumes from recruitment agencies 

Rest will review applications prior to the closing date and may close the role earlier

Job Description


Sydney or Melbourne 
2nd LOD

Established in 1988, Rest is one of Australia’s largest profit-to-member superannuation funds.

We support more than two million members, with around $100 billion of funds under management and are recognised as a responsible investment leader*. We believe when members understand and engage with their super, they’re more likely to get a better retirement outcome.

Everything we do at Rest is underpinned by our values and behaviours, we want to Be Daring, Keep it Simple, Take Action and Have Grit. To put it simply we want our people to thrive and love the work they do.

The Head of Information & Transformation Risk works with the General Manager, Operational Risk & Resilience to design, develop, implement, maintain and monitor Rest’s Enterprise Risk Management Framework (including assurance activities) as it relates to Information Technology, Information Security and Project Delivery risks, demonstrating and embodying a sound risk culture, and meeting Rest’s legislative and regulatory obligations.

Lead and manage the Information & Transformation Risk Team to develop capability, build a high performing team, foster collaboration, and increase engagement.
Provide independent and effective challenge to Data, Technology & Delivery (DTD) teams and broader organisation over Rest activities and decisions that may affect Rest’s information & transformation risks, this includes risks associated with information technology, cyber security, project delivery and data.
Lead the design, implementation and maintenance of appropriate frameworks, processes and capability supporting the management and assurance over information & transformation risks at Rest, aligning to regulatory and legislative obligations, and Rest’s strategic objectives.
Lead Second Line controls testing, assurance and risk reviews related to DTD teams, Rest’s project portfolio and other broader enterprise business activities as required, to ensure adequate controls are implemented commensurate with risks, vulnerabilities, and threats.
Prepare and present regular risk reporting to Executive forums and Board/Committees to support delivery of business outcomes.

Qualifications


Current certifications relevant to Information Technology or Cyber Security, such as CISSP, CISA or others as relevant (highly desired)  
Experience in senior risk, compliance or audit roles (primarily focused on Information Technology, Cyber Security and/or Project Delivery)
Experience leading risk advisory and assurance over large projects and transformation programs
Technical knowledge of industry best practices and emerging trends in risk management, technology, cyber security, project delivery, and artificial intelligence
Knowledge of frameworks and standards relevant to risk management, technology, cyber security, project delivery, and artificial intelligence (e.g. COBIT, NIST, ISO 27001, Agile, Voluntary AI Safety Standard)
Knowledge of the superannuation industry and legislative / regulatory obligations relevant to risk management, technology, and cyber security, including SPS 220, CPS 230, CPS 234, SOCI
Experience drafting high quality papers for Senior Leaders, Executives, Boards and Committees

Additional Information


Our benefits have been designed so you can tailor your experience with us and include:

Personal and professional development opportunities 
Hybrid working 
Purchase leave scheme and gender neutral 16 weeks paid parental leave 
Super Contribution Continuation for 12 Months of parental leave 
Linkedin Learning 
Income Protection Insurance 
Rest Excellence awards (peer recognition awards based on Rest’s values and behaviours) 
Rest Stops - meeting free breaks 

If you share our values, believe you can help make a difference for our members and want to be part of a leading superannuation fund with a Super culture, please click Apply Now.
